title: Normalize cache keys to maximize hit ratio
description: Strip irrelevant query params, headers, and cookies from the cache key so equivalent requests share a cached object.
body: Use when hit ratio is low or objects are duplicated across keys. Do not include tracking params, volatile headers, or all cookies in the key — each variation forks a separate cached object. Actions: define the cache key from only the fields that change the response, drop marketing/query noise, and constrain Vary to meaningful headers. Validation signals include a higher hit ratio and fewer distinct cached objects for the same content. Risk boundary: over-normalizing (dropping a header that does change the response) serves wrong content — verify the response truly is invariant to dropped fields.
